input.headline: George Washington faces Georgetown on 3-game losing streak

raw.headline: George Washington faces Georgetown on 3-game losing streak

winner: Why did George Washington refuse to play basketball against Georgetown? He was already on a 3-game losing streak with his ancestors.

candidates.text: George Washington's losing streak is so long, he hasn't won a battle since crossing the Delaware... and that was just his team's halftime score.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and uses a clever historical reference with a modern twist, creating surprise and humor."}

candidates.text: George Washington faces Georgetown on a 3-game losing streak — just don't ask him about the cherry trees.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on cherry trees tie in with Washington's history, adding surprise and specificity while keeping the joke concise and natural."}

candidates.text: George Washington lost three in a row—turns out his famous 'can't tell a lie' policy was more about basketball than cherry trees.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'can't tell a lie' with basketball context, specific to the headline, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: Georgetown's three-game winning streak just got *Washington*-ed.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'Washington' and the team name, concise and specific to the headline."}

candidates.text: Why did George Washington refuse to play basketball against Georgetown? He was already on a 3-game losing streak with his ancestors.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the historical reference and pun on 'losing streak,' but it's a bit forced and lacks natural flow. The connection between George Washington and Georgetown is tenuous, reducing surprise and specificity."}

candidates.text: George Washington is facing Georgetown on a three-game losing streak — which means he’s now leading a team that has lost more games than he ever won.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on historical figures with a modern sports context, concise and specific."}

candidates.text: George Washington is starting to realize that the only thing he can't conquer is a three-game losing streak.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and uses historical reference with a modern twist, but the humor is mild and relies on a somewhat predictable play on 'conquer'."}

candidates.text: It seems George Washington is struggling to keep his head above water, but he still hasn't figured out how to cross the Delaware in three tries.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke connects George Washington with a sports context, which is somewhat surprising, but the reference to crossing the Delaware is too vague and historical for the sports scenario, reducing clarity and impact."}

candidates.text: George Washington was devastated to find that he had to fight a small city instead of a revolutionary army.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and uses historical figures, but the humor is weak and relies on a forced pun rather than a clever twist."}
